ACODE Benchmarking Summit
9 and 10 July 2026
The 2026 ACODE Benchmarking Summit at the University of Melbourne, is one of our premier events focused on advancing technology-enhanced learning (TEL) within higher education. This summit offers a unique platform for educational leaders, faculty, and industry experts to collaborate, share knowledge, and engage in professional development.
Registrations for this event have now formally closed. If you would still like to get involved please don’t hesitate to contact us at secretariat@acode.edu.au .
What are the ACODE TEL Benchmarks?
The ACODE TEL Benchmarks are not standards but are designed to complement them, providing institutions with a comprehensive view of their TEL practices. Benchmarking helps institutions understand the extent to which they are applying standards and other measures to maintain quality across their educational offerings. Unlike standards, which operate at the operational level, benchmarking offers a broader, strategic view of TEL performance within the institution.
The ACODE TEL Benchmarks cover the following areas:
Institution-wide policy and governance for technology-enhanced learning
Planning for institution-wide quality improvement in TEL
Information technology systems, services, and support for TEL
The application of TEL services
Staff professional development for effective TEL use
Staff support for TEL integration
Student training for the effective use of TEL
Student support for the use of TEL
Technology-enhanced learning spaces
Accessible technology enhanced learning
We are excited to announce the development of the 10th benchmark on Accessibility , which will be unveiled at the 2026 summit.

Get ready for a milestone year at AIEC 2026 in Sydney, celebrating 40 years of advancing international education!
From 27–30 October , the international education sector will come together for four days of networking, learning, and celebration like no other.
The theme , “Future Thinking” , invites us to look ahead with imagination and purpose. From artificial intelligence and emerging technologies to new models of mobility and collaboration, the forces reshaping education are accelerating. AIEC 2026 will explore how the sector can anticipate global shifts, embrace innovation, and create more sustainable, inclusive, and connected pathways for learners and institutions worldwide.
2026 will see a refreshed conference format to make it more engaging and valuable than ever. Every concurrent session will be designed to be truly worth attending, offering interactive experiences, expert panels, and presentations that inspire and challenge ideas. Networking opportunities and social events will provide plenty of ways to connect, share insights, and expand your professional network with colleagues from Australia and overseas.
Join us in honouring the past, embracing the present, and envisioning a bold future for international education. Together, we’ll share ideas, explore new approaches, and prepare the sector for the challenges and opportunities of tomorrow. Future Thinking is about building the future of international education — together.

ASCILITE Spring into Excellence Research School
The ASCILITE Spring into Excellence Research School takes place annually and is hosted by a different institution each year.
The ASCILITE Spring into Excellence Research School is typically held over three (3) days in which academic and professional staff come together to further develop their research skills. The school is facilitated by senior researchers and leaders drawn from the ASCILITE community. It includes lunches, morning and afternoon teas, as well as expert presenters for a cost of $300 (ASCILITE current financial members) or $450 (non-members, includes a 12 month membership). The Research School commences at lunchtime day one – Monday, and finishes at around 1pm on day three – Wednesday.

Learning and teaching at Bond
A personalised learning experience
As Australia’s first private, not-for-profit university, we have always placed students at the centre of who we are, what we do, and how we do it. At the heart of our individualised approach to learning are our unique teaching methods, providing an innovative approach to education that enhances our student experience and student learning.
This learning experience is delivered by our world-class academic staff, who are supported by specialist teams dedicated to coaching, mentoring, and building and sustaining institutional capacity in learning and teaching.

Date and time: Thursday 28th November, 2024 2.00pm to 6.00pm AEDT
Hosted by: NSW Branch
Join us for the 2024 HERDSA NSW Branch Event on Curriculum, Assessment & AI: Real World Applications. This exciting event will take place at ACAP in Sydney CBD and online, bringing together leading academic educators to explore the transformative role of AI in education and innovation in assessment and curriculum. The conference features a stellar lineup of presentations, including our keynote address by Prof. Danny Liu from the University of Sydney. He will delve into the crucial topic of ‘Practically Responding to Generative AI and Assessment: What Are the Five Things We Need to Get Right?’, providing invaluable insights for educators navigating this evolving landscape. With sessions covering groundbreaking research on online assessments, storytelling in higher education, and managing academic integrity in the age of AI, attendees will leave inspired and equipped with practical strategies to enhance their teaching practices. Network with thought leaders and peers fostering connections that will extend beyond the event. For those attending on campus, afternoon tea will be provided.

Each year, the ISSOTL conference brings together scholars from across countries and disciplines, creating a vibrant space for sharing insights, sparking collaboration, and advancing the global conversation on teaching and learning.

News and events
Loading...
New national data shows universities must rethink the student experience
New national research led by Professor Chi Baik is highlighting the need for universities to reconsider how they support student wellbeing, belonging and engagement. The findings show students experiencing high psychological distress are more likely to question the value of their degree, consider dropping out, and report lower satisfaction with university life. The research is helping shape national conversations about creating more inclusive, supportive and meaningful higher education experiences for students.
Read full report

Featured content
Assessment, AI and Academic Integrity
This site has been developed to provide staff with practical ideas and strategies for improving assessment design and integrity, with the focus on evidencing student learning.
Internationalisation of the curriculum
This new resource is designed to provide staff with an overview of strategies, practical tools, ideas, and examples for adding international or intercultural elements to the curricula.
Student wellbeing and university experiences
Our research on student wellbeing in higher education aims to foster and sustain university students' wellbeing and academic achievement.
Indigenous history of the University project
This project examines the relationship between Indigenous Australia and the University of Melbourne from its foundation in 1853 until the present.
Joining CSHE community
The CSHE community thrives on the diverse expertise and perspectives of our graduate researchers, visiting scholars, fellows, and research associates.
Together, they create a vibrant intellectual ecosystem where innovative ideas flourish and collaborative efforts shape the future of higher education.

Queensland and Victoria’s premier K-12 curriculum focused education event
Inspiring professional development for Australian educators
The National Education Summit has been a key professional development event for Australian educators since 2016, continually evolving to offer fresh ideas and practical strategies for teachers and school leaders. In 2026, the Summit will feature a range of Conferences, hands-on Workshops, and interactive Seminars led by respected experts and thought leaders in education.
Alongside these learning opportunities, attendees can explore The Education Show — a free trade expo showcasing the latest educational resources, technologies, and support services. It’s a chance to connect directly with exhibitors, discover new tools for the classroom, and gain valuable insights while earning professional development hours.

Keynote presenter
Professor Jason M. Lodge, PFHEA – Professor of Educational Psychology and Director of the Learning, Instruction, and Technology Lab in the School of Education at The University of Queensland, Australia.
His work explores the cognitive and emotional mechanisms of learning with digital technologies, addressing critical questions of how technology, particularly AI, is shaping learning and education. Jason’s research informs educational policy and practice across Australia and internationally. He serves as an expert advisor for the Australian Government and OECD, applying his work to enhance equitable learning for all students.

This Teaching and Learning website provides key information and ideas about teaching at the University of Tasmania. This site provides information, ideas and examples of MyLO use for teaching and learning in the context of the different elements of learning design. For an introduction to MyLO as a system, including information about its functions and how to use its tools, please visit the MyLO Staff Resources page .
If you are looking for information about workshops and events for University of Tasmania staff, please visit the Professional Learning intranet site (University staff access only).

Welcome to Learning and Teaching at Western.
"The purpose of teaching is simple: It is to make student learning possible." – Paul Ramsden
We are here to provide leadership, expertise, support, and recognition in Learning and Teaching across Western. Our mission is to empower educators to deliver high-quality teaching in a rapidly changing world, and our purpose is to make student learning and success possible.
Learning and Teaching Support
Blackboard Ultra
Get comfortable with our new Learning Management System (LMS), Blackboard Ultra. Find out about the guiding principles of Ultra and join a workshop to learn more.
Teaching Quality Framework
Learn about our new Teaching Quality Framework (TQF), and how we aim to develop a robust and scholarly teaching culture that is supported, valued, recognised and rewarded.
Professional Development
Find out about the range of formal and informal programs that we offer for academic and professional staff to develop their teaching and career.
EducationHQ
Login to our internal Learning and Teaching website to access all the latest information, resources, and news, relating to Learning and Teaching at Western.
